sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After clicking the top-center white tile, it turned green. So the mechanic is confirmed:
- White tiles toggle: white → green → white
- Gray tiles toggle: gray → green → gray
The right grid now shows:
```
gray  green  gray
white gray   white
gray  white  gray
```
So the pattern is building up. The target from the left grid is a cross (4 purple dots at edge centers). The right grid has white tiles at those same positions. I need to turn all 4 white tiles green to match the pattern.
Current state: 1 of 4 white tiles is green (top-center). Need to turn the other 3 white tiles green:
- (1,0) left-center: row 55, col 25
- (1,2) right-center: row 55, col 35
- (2,1) bottom-center: row 60, col 30
Let me click all 3 remaining white tiles to turn them green.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: White tiles toggle white↔green. The right grid has white tiles at the cross positions matching the left grid's purple pattern. Need to turn all 4 white tiles green to complete the pattern. Currently 1/4 is green.
Plan: Click the remaining 3 white tiles to turn them green.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the 3 remaining white tiles to turn them green
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},   # left-center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},   # right-center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},   # bottom-center
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
